lessons.name: OpenAI — what their values look like in interviews
Anthropic — values and how they probe in interviews
Palantir — values, with a focus on failure stories

existing_cards.q: STAR weighting for Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) behavioral rounds?
existing_cards.a: Situation: 2-3 sentences MAX. Task: clarify YOUR ownership (FDE rounds probe individual accountability). Action: ~60% of the story, focus on technical + customer decisions you made. Result: both technical outcome and quantified business impact.

existing_cards.q: Five must-have stories for a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) loop?
existing_cards.a: (1) Production fix under live customer pressure. (2) Pushing back on a client request. (3) Owning a deployment failure end-to-end. (4) Explaining a hard technical limit to a non-technical stakeholder. (5) Making a decision with incomplete information.
Real, specific, owned, with the actual mechanism + cost + systemic change you made afterward. "I work too hard" / "I helped with..." are instant-reject signals.

existing_cards.q: OpenAI Charter principles and 2025/2026 context to know for an interview?
existing_cards.a: Four Charter principles: Broadly Distributed Benefits, Long-Term Safety, Technical Leadership, Cooperative Orientation. As of Oct 2025, OpenAI restructured: the for-profit is now OpenAI Group PBC under the OpenAI Foundation (nonprofit). Have a specific reason for the Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) role, not just 'OpenAI'.

AI safety, beneficial AI, Constitutional AI, and the Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP v3.0, Feb 2026) — which moved to a two-track model (Anthropic-only mitigations + industry-wide map) and added a public Frontier Safety Roadmap. Expect ethical-dilemma scenarios; surface depth on safety, not generic concern.
Pull-not-push: focus on what attracts you to the new role; light positive about current. Never badmouth — signals you'll do the same at the new place in a year.

existing_cards.q: "Disagree and commit" — what most candidates miss?
existing_cards.a: The COMMIT half. Telling a story where you disagreed AND won is incomplete. Stronger: disagreed, brought data, lost, executed fully, validated the team's view in hindsight.
(1) Production fix under pressure. (2) Pushing back on a customer. (3) Deployment failure ownership. (4) Explaining a technical limit. (5) Decision under incomplete info.

existing_cards.q: Biggest behavioral red flags in a Palantir interview?
existing_cards.a: (1) Fake-failure dodges ('I work too hard', 'I don't really fail'). (2) Surface-level 'why Palantir' — citing comp/perks/'cool problems' instead of specifics. (3) Mixing up Foundry (commercial) and Gotham (gov). (4) Inability to justify a technical choice. Cultural-fit signals weigh heavily; they reject strong technical candidates over these.
Pull-not-push: what attracts you forward; light positive on current employer. Never badmouth — signals you'll do same about them later.

existing_cards.q: STAR weighting for FDE — biggest section?
existing_cards.a: Action ~60% of the story. Be specific about both technical and interpersonal moves you made.
(1) Production fix under pressure. (2) Pushing back on customer. (3) Deployment failure ownership. (4) Explaining a technical limit. (5) Decision under incomplete info.

Charter still anchors on AGI that benefits humanity; updated April 2026 to five operational principles: Democratization, Empowerment, Universal Prosperity, Resilience, Adaptability. Culture also stresses intensity + scale + shipping products people love. Have a specific reason for the role, not a generic mission quote.

Real engagement with safety: RSP v3.0 (Feb 2026, replaces hard pauses with a public Frontier Safety Roadmap + recurring risk assessments), Constitutional AI, and the Acceptable Use Policy. Expect ethical-dilemma scenarios; generic 'I do the right thing' answers fail.

existing_cards.q: "Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager" — what most candidates skip?
existing_cards.a: The COMMIT half: executing fully on the team's decision, even when you lost. Often stronger when you were partly wrong + did your share.

existing_cards.q: "Tell me about mentoring someone" — what makes it strong?
existing_cards.a: Specific person + specific gap + your approach + measurable outcome (promotion, project shipped, observable behavior change).
